records.ety: [OE. sacrifise, sacrifice, F. sacrifice, fr. L. sacrificium; sacer sacred + facere to make. See Sacred, and Fact.]

defs: The offering of anything to God, or to a god; consecratory rite.
quotes: Great pomp, and sacrifice, and praises loud, To Dagon.

defs: Anything consecrated and offered to God, or to a divinity; an immolated victim, or an offering of any kind, laid upon an altar, or otherwise presented in the way of religious thanksgiving, atonement, or conciliation.
quotes: Moloch, horrid king, besmeared with blood Of human sacrifice.
My life, if thou preserv'st my life, Thy sacrifice shall be.

defs: Destruction or surrender of anything for the sake of something else; devotion of some desirable object in behalf of a higher object, or to a claim deemed more pressing; hence, also, the thing so devoted or given up; as, the sacrifice of interest to pleasure, or of pleasure to interest.
A sale at a price less than the cost or the actual value.

records.ety: [From Sacrifice, n.: cf. F. sacrifier, L. sacrificare; sacer sacred, holy + -ficare (only in comp.) to make. See -fy.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Sacrificed (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Sacrificing (<?/).]

defs: To make an offering of; to consecrate or present to a divinity by way of expiation or propitiation, or as a token acknowledgment or thanksgiving; to immolate on the altar of God, in order to atone for sin, to procure favor, or to express thankfulness; as, to sacrifice an ox or a sheep.
quotes: Oft sacrificing bullock, lamb, or kid.

defs: Hence, to destroy, surrender, or suffer to be lost, for the sake of obtaining something; to give up in favor of a higher or more imperative object or duty; to devote, with loss or suffering.
quotes: Condemned to sacrifice his childish years To babbling ignorance, and to empty fears.
The Baronet had sacrificed a large sum . . . for the sake of . . . making this boy his heir.

defs: To sell at a price less than the cost or the actual value.
To make offerings to God, or to a deity, of things consumed on the altar; to offer sacrifice.
quotes: O teacher, some great mischief hath befallen To that meek man, who well had sacrificed.
